title: 10 best herbal creams for indigestion

remedies.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale creams helps with indigestion because they contain the active compound gingerol, which has potent anti-inflammatory and digestive properties. Ginger has long been used in traditional medicine to soothe the digestive system and alleviate symptoms of indigestion, such as bloating and nausea. The cream's topical application allows for quick absorption of ginger's benefits into the body, providing relief from indigestion and promoting overall digestive well-being. Regular use may also help prevent indigestion in the long term.
remedy_constituents: Gingerols: These compounds help with indigestion by reducing inflammation and relaxing the muscles in the digestive tract, which can alleviate symptoms of nausea and vomiting.
Shogaols: Similar to gingerols, shogaols have anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe digestive issues and alleviate indigestion symptoms, such as bloating and discomfort.

Terpenes (specifically zingiberene and bisabolol): Terpenes have been shown to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which can help reduce digestive inflammation and alleviate symptoms of indigestion, such as abdominal pain and cramping.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: These underground stems store nutrients and have anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe indigestion and alleviate digestive discomfort.
Roots: The roots of Zingiber officinale contain bioactive compounds that aid in digestion and provide relief from indigestion symptoms.
Stems: The stems of the rhyzome are often used in combination with the rhyzome itself to create a concentrated extract that can help alleviate indigestion and promote digestion.
remedy_recipe: Infuse 10 grams of fresh zingiber officinale in 100 milliliters of carrier oil for 2 weeks.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth and discard the solids after 2 weeks.
Combine the infused oil with 10 grams of beeswax and 20 grams of candelilla wax.
Heat the mixture in a double boiler at 180 degrees fahrenheit for 10 minutes.
Pour the mixture into a glass jar and let it cool and solidify at room temperature.

remedies.remedy_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra creams helps with indigestion because of its soothing and anti-inflammatory properties. The root of the licorice plant, Glycyrrhiza glabra, has been used for centuries to calm digestive issues. The cream's active compound, glycyrrhizin, reduces inflammation in the stomach lining, thereby alleviating symptoms of indigestion, such as heartburn and bloating. Additionally, Glycyrrhiza glabra creams have a mild antispasmodic effect, which helps to relax the muscles in the digestive tract, promoting a smoother digestive process and providing relief from indigestion.
remedy_constituents: Glycyrrhizin: Helps to reduce inflammation and soothe the mucous membranes in the digestive tract, alleviating symptoms of indigestion such as heartburn and bloating.
Flavonoids: Act as antioxidants and anti-inflammatory agents, protecting the digestive system from oxidative stress and inflammation caused by indigestion.
Licoricidin: Exhibits anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties, which help to relax the muscles in the digestive tract and reduce symptoms of indigestion such as cramping and spasms.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots contain glycyrrhizin, a compound that has been shown to have anti-inflammatory properties and help soothe digestive issues.
Leaves: The leaves of the plant are also known to contain glycyrrhizin, which contributes to their use in treating indigestion and other digestive issues.
Barks: The barks of Glycyrrhiza glabra are sometimes used to make creams due to their content of glycosides, which help alleviate digestive issues and inflammation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 50 grams of dried glycyrrhiza glabra roots and dry them further in a low-temperature oven at 60°c for 24 hours.
Grind the dried roots into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle for 10 minutes.
Combine 20 grams of the ground glycyrrhiza glabra powder with 100 grams of coconut oil in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture in the double boiler at 40°c for 30 minutes under gentle stirring every 5 minutes.
Strain the cooled mixture through a cheesecloth and store it in a glass container for up to 6 months.

remedies.remedy_desc: Foeniculum vulgare creams helps with indigestion because of its carminative properties, which aid in the relief of digestive discomfort. The essential oils present in Foeniculum vulgare, such as anethole, have a soothing effect on the stomach and intestines, reducing inflammation and spasms. Additionally, the cream's anti-inflammatory properties help to calm the digestive tract, alleviating symptoms of indigestion, bloating, and gas. By promoting digestion and reducing discomfort, Foeniculum vulgare creams provide natural relief from indigestion and promote overall digestive health.
remedy_constituents: Furanocoumarins: Help alleviate indigestion by preventing the growth of bacteria in the gut and reducing inflammation.
Phenolic acids: Assist in indigestion relief by inhibiting the production of stomach acid and reducing inflammation in the digestive tract.
Terpenoids: Contribute to indigestion management by relaxing the muscles in the digestive tract, reducing spasms, and improving digestion.
remedy_parts: Seeds: They are used to make creams for indigestion due to their carminative and antispasmodic properties.
Leaves: They are used to make creams for indigestion due to their anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties.
Fruits: They are used to make creams for indigestion due to their carminative and digestive properties.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 500 grams of foeniculum vulgare leaves and flowers at dawn to ensure optimal potency and aroma.
Steam distill the harvested foeniculum vulgare for 4 hours to extract essential oil.
Mix 200 grams of shea butter with 100 grams of beeswax and 50 grams of coconut oil.
Combine 100 grams of the foeniculum vulgare essential oil with 50 grams of jojoba oil and 20 grams of vitamin e oil.
Blend the oil mixture with the shea butter mixture and pour into jars for storage.

remedies.remedy_desc: Cinchona officinalis creams helps with indigestion because of its anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties. The creams often contain quinine, a natural compound found in the Cinchona tree, which helps to relax the muscles in the digestive tract and reduce inflammation. This can help to alleviate symptoms of indigestion, such as bloating and cramps. Additionally, Cinchona officinalis creams may also help to improve digestion by increasing blood flow to the stomach and promoting the release of digestive enzymes, ultimately providing relief from indigestion and discomfort.
remedy_constituents: Quinoline Alkaloids: These compounds have anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties, which help to reduce inflammation and relax the muscles in the digestive tract, alleviating symptoms of indigestion.
Quinic Acid: This compound has a soothing effect on the digestive system, helping to calm irritation and reduce inflammation in the stomach and intestines, thereby providing relief from indigestion.

Flavonoids: These compounds have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to protect the stomach lining from damage and reduce inflammation, thereby alleviating symptoms of indigestion.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Alkaloids extracted from the leaves are used to make creams for indigestion due to their quinine content, which helps to alleviate symptoms.
Barks: The barks of the Cinchona officinalis tree contain quinine and other alkaloids that are used to make creams for indigestion.
Stems: Alkaloids from the stems are also used in creams for indigestion due to their quinine content, which helps to soothe digestive issues.
remedy_recipe: Gently combine 100 grams of dried cinchona officinalis bark with 500 milliliters of carrier oil in a glass jar.
Steep the mixture for 2 weeks in a cool dark place with occasional shaking.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into another container.
Mix 10 grams of beeswax with 20 grams of shea butter and 10 milliliters of the infused oil.
Heat the mixture for 5 minutes in a double boiler or a heat-proof bowl set over boiling water.

remedies.remedy_desc: Mentha x piperita creams helps with indigestion because they contain the active compound menthol, which has natural anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties. When applied topically, menthol can relax the abdominal muscles and reduce cramping, making it easier to digest food. Additionally, menthol's cooling sensation can help reduce bloating and discomfort associated with indigestion. This natural remedy has been used for centuries to provide relief from indigestion and alleviate discomfort, making it a popular choice for those seeking a gentle and effective solution.
remedy_constituents: Menthol: Helps to ease indigestion by reducing inflammation in the digestive tract and relaxing muscles in the stomach and intestines.
Piperitone: Provides relief from indigestion by reducing nausea and vomiting through its antispasmodic and anti-inflammatory properties.
Linalool: Relieves indigestion symptoms by reducing inflammation and promoting digestion through its anti-inflammatory and carminative effects.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Used for their carminative and antispasmodic properties to help alleviate digestive issues.
Stems: Used for their menthol content, which helps to calm digestive discomfort and relieve nausea.
Roots: Used for their bitter compounds, which stimulate digestive enzymes and help to soothe indigestion.
remedy_recipe: Infuse 100g of fresh mentha x piperita leaves in 500ml of boiling water for 10 minutes.
Strain the liquid and discard the solids to obtain a fragrant herbal tea.
Mix 150g of beeswax and 50g of shea butter in a double boiler to melt.
Combine 100ml of the herbal tea with 50ml of distilled water and add to the melted wax mixture.
Pour the cream into clean containers and store in the refrigerator for up to 6 months.

remedies.remedy_desc: Curcuma longa creams helps with indigestion because they contain curcumin, a bioactive compound with potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. Curcumin has been shown to reduce inflammation in the digestive tract, alleviate symptoms of indigestion such as bloating and cramps, and improve digestion by increasing the production of digestive enzymes. Additionally, curcumin has been found to have a protective effect on the stomach lining, reducing irritation and allowing the stomach to function more efficiently, providing relief from indigestion and its associated discomfort.
remedy_constituents: Curcuminoids: They help with indigestion by reducing inflammation in the digestive tract and alleviating symptoms such as bloating and discomfort.
Tannins: They help with indigestion by tightening the tissues in the stomach and reducing inflammation, which can help alleviate heartburn and acid reflux.
Gingerols: They help with indigestion by reducing inflammation and relaxing the muscles in the digestive tract, which can help alleviate nausea, vomiting, and stomach discomfort.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: Rhyzomes are the most commonly used part of Curcuma longa in making creams for indigestion due to their high concentration of curcumin, a compound with anti-inflammatory properties.
Roots: Roots of Curcuma longa are also used in making creams for indigestion, as they contain curcumin and other bioactive compounds that help alleviate digestive issues.

Stems: Some preparations may utilize stems of Curcuma longa to make creams for indigestion, though this is less common, as the bioactive compounds are less concentrated in the stems compared to the rhyzomes and roots.
remedy_recipe: Weigh 100g of dried curcuma longa rhizomes and grind them into a fine powder using a spice grinder.
Mix the ground curcuma longa powder with 200g of beeswax and 100g of coconut oil in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture in the double boiler over low heat for 30 minutes or until the beeswax melts completely.
Remove the double boiler from the heat and let it cool slightly before adding 10 drops of vitamin e oil and 10 drops of lavender oil.
Whip the mixture with a hand mixer or whisk until it thickens into a smooth and creamy consistency.

remedies.remedy_desc: Cinnamomum verum creams helps with indigestion because they contain the essential oils of true cinnamon, which have natural anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties. These properties help to reduce inflammation in the digestive tract and relax the muscles in the stomach, alleviating symptoms of indigestion such as bloating, cramping, and nausea. Additionally, the warm and spicy aroma of Cinnamomum verum creams can stimulate digestion and relieve discomfort, making them a popular remedy for indigestion and other digestive issues.
remedy_constituents: Cinnamaldehyde: It helps with indigestion by relaxing the stomach muscles, reducing inflammation, and improving digestion.
Cinnamic acid: It aids in indigestion by inhibiting the growth of bacteria that cause gastrointestinal issues and reducing inflammation in the digestive tract.
Eugenol: It helps with indigestion by reducing inflammation, relieving pain, and inhibiting the production of stomach acid, which can alleviate symptoms of indigestion.
remedy_parts: Barks: The barks of Cinnamomum verum contain oils and compounds that have anti-inflammatory and soothing properties, which can help ease indigestion symptoms when incorporated into creams.
Leaves: The leaves of Cinnamomum verum contain compounds with anti-inflammatory and carminative properties, making them useful in creams for reducing indigestion and discomfort.

Rhyzomes: The rhyzomes of Cinnamomum verum have been traditionally used for their digestive and anti-inflammatory properties, which can be beneficial in creams for treating indigestion and related issues.
remedy_recipe: Extract 50 grams of dried cinnamomum verum bark from a reputable source and grind it into a fine powder using a spice grinder for 5 minutes.
Combine the ground cinnamomum verum powder with 100 grams of coconut oil in a double boiler over low heat for 30 minutes.
Add 20 grams of beeswax to the coconut oil mixture and stir until it is fully incorporated and melted after 10 minutes.
Remove the double boiler from the heat and let it cool for 10 minutes then whip the mixture with a hand mixer until it thickens and becomes smooth after 5 minutes.
Transfer the herbal cinnamomum verum cream to a clean glass jar and store it in the refrigerator to chill for at least 2 hours before use.

remedies.remedy_desc: Aloe vera creams helps with indigestion because of its soothing and anti-inflammatory properties. The gel extracted from aloe vera plants contains compounds like aloe-emodin and aloin, which help to calm the digestive tract and reduce inflammation. When applied topically, aloe vera creams can provide quick relief from heartburn, bloating, and discomfort associated with indigestion. The natural cooling effect of aloe vera also helps to neutralize stomach acid, promoting a sense of comfort and ease. This natural remedy can be a valuable addition to a digestive health routine.
remedy_constituents: Anthraquinones: These compounds help alleviate indigestion by reducing inflammation in the digestive tract and relaxing the muscles to ease digestion.
Phenolic acids: Phenolic acids in Aloe vera help protect the stomach lining from acid damage, reducing irritation and discomfort associated with indigestion.
Glycoproteins: Glycoproteins in Aloe vera creams have anti-inflammatory properties, which help soothe the digestive tract and reduce symptoms of indigestion such as bloating and cramps.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The gel from Aloe vera leaves is often used to make creams and ointments for indigestion relief due to its soothing and anti-inflammatory properties.
Aloe Vera Juice (from leaves): Aloe vera juice made from the leaves is used in creams and ointments to help alleviate indigestion symptoms by soothing the digestive tract and promoting healing.
remedy_recipe: Gather ingredients including 1 cup aloe vera gel, 1/2 cup coconut oil, 1/4 cup beeswax, 2 tablespoons vitamin e oil and 2 teaspoons essential oil.
Mix aloe vera gel and coconut oil in a bowl until well combined taking 5 minutes to prepare.
Melt beeswax in a double boiler over low heat for 10 minutes then add to the aloe vera mixture.
Stir in vitamin e oil and essential oil into the mixture thoroughly until well combined taking 3 minutes.
Pour the mixture into containers and let it cool and solidify at room temperature for 30 minutes.

remedies.remedy_desc: Zanthoxylum bungeanum creams helps with indigestion because they contain a unique combination of natural compounds that soothe and calm the digestive system. The anti-inflammatory properties of Zanthoxylum bungeanum help to reduce inflammation and irritation in the stomach and intestines, alleviating symptoms of indigestion such as bloating and discomfort. Additionally, the herb's carminative properties help to ease digestion and relieve gas, making it an effective natural remedy for indigestion and promoting overall digestive health. This helps in preventing further complications.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: These compounds help alleviate indigestion by reducing inflammation and improving digestion through their antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties.
Catechins: Catechins help with indigestion by inhibiting the growth of bacteria that can contribute to digestive issues and reducing inflammation in the gastrointestinal tract.
Coumarins: Coumarins help with indigestion by exhibiting anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can help reduce digestive discomfort and promote a healthy gut environment.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: They are used due to their carminative properties, helping to alleviate digestive issues such as bloating and indigestion.
Seeds: Their essential oils and compounds help in reducing inflammation and soothing digestive issues, making them a popular choice for indigestion creams.
Barks: The barks of Zanthoxylum bungeanum are used to create creams for indigestion due to their ability to stimulate digestion and relieve symptoms of indigestion and bloating.
remedy_recipe: Extract 500g dried zanthoxylum bungeanum bark in 2 liters of 95 ethanol for 2 weeks.
Filter the mixture and obtain 200ml of concentrated zanthoxylum bungeanum extract.
Combine 100g of the extract with 500g of beeswax in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture until the beeswax melts and mix with 200ml of distilled water.
Pour the mixture into small containers and allow it to cool and solidify completely.

remedies.remedy_desc: Aloe barbadensis creams helps with indigestion because of its soothing and anti-inflammatory properties. The gel from the aloe vera plant contains compounds such as aloin and aloe-emodin, which have been shown to reduce inflammation in the digestive tract and promote healing of the mucous membranes. This can help to alleviate symptoms of indigestion such as heartburn and bloating. Additionally, aloe vera has natural antacid properties, which can help to neutralize stomach acid and provide quick relief from indigestion.
remedy_constituents: Aloe-emodin: Aloe-emodin helps with indigestion by reducing inflammation and inhibiting the growth of bacteria that can cause gastrointestinal issues.
Aloin: Aloin helps with indigestion by soothing the digestive tract, reducing irritation, and promoting the healing of ulcers and other gastrointestinal wounds.
Saponins: Saponins help with indigestion by reducing inflammation, preventing the formation of ulcers, and improving the overall gut health by promoting the growth of beneficial gut bacteria.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Aloe vera gel, extracted from the leaves, is used in creams to soothe and calm digestive issues due to its anti-inflammatory properties.
Gel from the leaves: The gel has anti-inflammatory and soothing properties that help to ease digestion and alleviate symptoms of indigestion.
Alginic acid from the leaves: Alginic acid, a component found in the gel, helps to reduce inflammation and soothe digestive issues, making it a key ingredient in creams for indigestion.
remedy_recipe: Weigh 100 grams of aloe barbadensis gel and transfer it to a blender for processing.
Add 20 grams of vegetable glycerin and blend the mixture for 2 minutes at high speed.
Transfer the blended mixture to a double boiler and heat it to 70 degrees celsius for 15 minutes.
Mix in 10 grams of beeswax and 10 grams of coconut oil and stir until fully incorporated.
Pour the mixture into a mold and refrigerate it at 4 degrees celsius for 30 minutes.

intro_desc: Herbal creams for indigestion are topical ointments that help alleviate digestive discomfort by targeting the skin and underlying tissues, rather than the digestive system directly. They contain a blend of herbs such as Zingiber officinale, Glycyrrhiza glabra, Foeniculum vulgare, Cinchona officinalis, Mentha x piperita, Curcuma longa, Cinnamomum verum, Aloe vera, Zanthoxylum bungeanum, and Aloe barbadensis. If left untreated, indigestion can lead to chronic pain, anxiety, difficulty sleeping, and a reduced quality of life, affecting daily activities, relationships, and overall well-being.
